report_name: Medtronic Data Breach Exposes Personal and Health Data of 3.8 Million

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A prolific extortion group that claimed responsibility for the Medtronic data breach. The group allegedly gained unauthorized access to the corporate IT environment and exfiltrated terabytes of data, including the personal and health information of 3.8 million individuals, for financial extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters exfiltrated large volumes of sensitive data to their own servers as part of their typical modus operandi.

attack_patterns.name: Local Data Staging
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely created database dump files (.sql, .bak) on the local system to stage sensitive data before exfiltration.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: The command line pattern 'rclone copy /local/path remote:bucket' was noted as a method for exfiltrating large amounts of data to cloud storage.

identities.name: Medtronic
identities.description: A major medical technology company that suffered a data breach in April 2026, resulting in the theft of personal and health information for approximately 3.8 million individuals.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by ShinyHunters to publicly announce the Medtronic breach and pressure the company into paying a ransom.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Exfiltration Servers
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled servers used to store the terabytes of sensitive data exfiltrated from Medtronic's corporate IT environment.

indicators.name: Rclone Execution for Data Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Rclone Execution for Data Exfiltration
id: e1b2c3d4-a5f6-4b90-8cde-f1234567890a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the rclone tool with the 'copy' command, a common TTP for exfiltrating large amounts of data to cloud storage.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/medtronic-data-breach-impacts-3-8-million-people/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'copy'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the rclone tool with the 'copy' command, a common TTP for exfiltrating large amounts of data to cloud storage.

indicators.name: Database Dump File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Database Dump File Creation
id: f2c3d4e5-b6a7-4891-9def-12345678901b
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of database dump files (.sql, .bak), which often precedes data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/medtronic-data-breach-impacts-3-8-million-people/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1074.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.sql'
      - '.bak'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\sqlserver.exe'
      - '\sqlagent.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the creation of database dump files (.sql, .bak), which often precedes data exfiltration.
